dialogue_line_replacements.text: Me ducho en diez minutos, me visto rápido y me maquillo en el camino.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: I shower in ten minutes, dress quickly and do my makeup on the way.

dialogue_line_replacements.text: Sí, él siempre se levanta primero y se ducha antes que yo.
dialogue_line_replacements.translation: Yes, he always gets up first and showers before me. (Spain: ducharse more common)

cultural_notes_overrides.formality: In Spain, 'ducharse' is more common than 'bañarse' for showering. 'Bañarse' specifically implies a bath (baño de bañera).
cultural_notes_overrides.gestures: Morning routines in Spain often start later — many Spaniards don't eat breakfast at home and get ready quickly.
cultural_notes_overrides.regional_variations: Spain uses 'ducharse' for showering. 'El baño' is both the bathroom and a bath. 'Lavarse los dientes' (brush teeth) uses the same reflexive structure across all dialects.
